learningObjectives.title: Understand real-time tracking integration
learningObjectives.description: Explain GPS, IoT, and telematics data ingestion into SAP TM.

learningObjectives.title: Understand monitored data types
learningObjectives.description: Describe location, cargo conditions, vehicle state, and event information.

learningObjectives.title: Understand proactive exception handling
learningObjectives.description: Clarify how alerts and business rules convert sensor data into actions.

sections.content: SAP Transportation Management integrates with GPS devices, IoT sensors and telematics platforms to provide real-time shipment visibility. These technologies continuously transmit vehicle location, shipment conditions and transportation events to SAP TM, enabling proactive monitoring, exception management and better customer service.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: GPS tells where the vehicle is, IoT tells the condition of the shipment and telematics provides operational vehicle information. SAP TM uses this data for transportation visibility.

A refrigerated truck transporting vaccines continuously sends GPS location and temperature readings to SAP TM. During transit, the temperature rises above the acceptable limit. SAP TM immediately creates an exception, alerts the planner and notifies the carrier before product quality is affected.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Configure automatic alerts for temperature deviations, route deviations and unexpected stops so transportation planners can respond immediately to critical situations.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A pharmaceutical company integrated SAP TM with IoT temperature sensors and GPS-enabled trucks. Cold-chain compliance improved significantly while spoilage claims were reduced by more than 40%.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Know the distinction: GPS provides location, IoT monitors shipment conditions and telematics provides vehicle operational data. SAP TM combines all three for intelligent transportation monitoring.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: GPS tracking alone does not guarantee transportation visibility. SAP TM also requires event processing, business rules and exception management to convert raw tracking data into meaningful logistics information.

items: IoT sensors monitor shipment conditions such as temperature and humidity.
SAP TM uses this information for event tracking and exception management.
Integration improves shipment visibility, security and customer service.
